verses.text: Lay aside malice and guile; 'as newborn babes, desire the sincere milk of the word,' having tasted the Lord is gracious. Crave the word. The reborn long to grow by Scripture.
Lay aside malice and guile; 'as newborn babes, desire the sincere milk of the word,' having tasted the Lord is gracious. Crave the word. The reborn long to grow by Scripture.
Lay aside malice and guile; 'as newborn babes, desire the sincere milk of the word,' having tasted the Lord is gracious. Crave the word. The reborn long to grow by Scripture.
Coming to Christ, 'a living stone,' you are built up 'a spiritual house'; 'he that believeth on him shall not be confounded.' Living temple. Believers are stones in God's house.
Coming to Christ, 'a living stone,' you are built up 'a spiritual house'; 'he that believeth on him shall not be confounded.' Living temple. Believers are stones in God's house.
Coming to Christ, 'a living stone,' you are built up 'a spiritual house'; 'he that believeth on him shall not be confounded.' Living temple. Believers are stones in God's house.
To you He is precious; to the disobedient, 'the stone which the builders disallowed' becomes 'a stone of stumbling.' Two responses. Christ is either cornerstone or stumbling-block.
To you He is precious; to the disobedient, 'the stone which the builders disallowed' becomes 'a stone of stumbling.' Two responses. Christ is either cornerstone or stumbling-block.
You are 'a chosen generation, a royal priesthood, an holy nation,' called out of darkness 'into his marvellous light.' New people. Once not a people, now God's own.
You are 'a chosen generation, a royal priesthood, an holy nation,' called out of darkness 'into his marvellous light.' New people. Once not a people, now God's own.
As 'strangers and pilgrims,' abstain from fleshly lusts; let your good works lead the Gentiles to glorify God. Honest walk. Pilgrims live so the world sees God.
As 'strangers and pilgrims,' abstain from fleshly lusts; let your good works lead the Gentiles to glorify God. Honest walk. Pilgrims live so the world sees God.
Submit to every ordinance for the Lord's sake, honouring the king; 'as free, and not using your liberty for a cloke of maliciousness.' Honour all. Freedom serves, not excuses, sin.

Servants, submit even to harsh masters; to suffer wrongfully and endure 'is acceptable with God.' Patient under wrong. God notes the suffering of the faithful.
Servants, submit even to harsh masters; to suffer wrongfully and endure 'is acceptable with God.' Patient under wrong. God notes the suffering of the faithful.
Servants, submit even to harsh masters; to suffer wrongfully and endure 'is acceptable with God.' Patient under wrong. God notes the suffering of the faithful.
Christ suffered for us, leaving 'an example,' who 'did no sin,' bore our sins on the tree, that 'by whose stripes ye were healed.' His pattern. The sinless Saviour suffered to heal us.
